headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, dominates this cycle as educators benchmark, demo, and price out its trade-offs.
body: The recommended channels lead with Claude Fable 5, which Better Stack reports tops SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code with vision and extended context for long-running tasks [4], while Cyril Imhof frames its use in regulated industries through a "Know Your Agent" context engineering method [5]. Beyond the inner circle, AI Search demos Fable 5 across image recognition, ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music, and medical imaging against GPT 5.5 [8], Jordan Urbs one-shots a video processing app in an hour but warns of elevated token burn and availability only through June 22 [9], and David Ondrej argues against running Fable 5 inside Claude [11]. The recommended set also covers production agent tooling, including Google's open-source Agents CLI with sandboxed execution and audit trails [1], Claude Code built PAI for persistent cross-session memory [2], and IBM's analysis of agent scaling cost and latency [7] plus AI worms and nonhuman identity risk [3]. Counterpoints arrive from NVIDIA's hybrid Mamba transformer Nemotron 3 with Latent MoE and MTP [0] and Snorkel's case that a sub-$500 finetuned 4B model beat Qwen 3 235B on a SQL task [12].
